hints.to be: That would be 'esse' — 'Stat' comes from 'stare' (to stand/be fixed)
hints.to place: That would be 'statuere' — 'Stat' comes from 'stare' (to stand)
hints.to remain: That would be 'manere' — 'Stat' comes from 'stare' (to stand/be fixed)

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'stetit' — 'stat' is present indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'stet' — 'stat' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'stabit' — 'stat' is present indicative

hints.sisto, sistere, stiti, statum: These are the parts of 'sistere' (to cause to stand/stop)
hints.statuo, statuere, statui, statutum: These are the parts of 'statuere' (to set up/decide)
hints.sum, esse, fui, futurus: These are the parts of 'esse' (to be)

steps.reveal: cuique = for each one (dat. sg. of quisque)

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'quoque' — 'cuique' is dative singular of 'quisque'

steps.reveal: dies = the day (nom. sg., subject of 'stat')

hints.accusative: Accusative singular is also 'diem' — 'dies' here is nominative, subject of 'stat'
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'die' — 'dies' is nominative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'diei' ends in -ei (5th declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'diei' ends in -ei

steps.reveal: tempus = time (nom. sg., second subject with 'dies')

hints.accusative: While 'tempus' can be accusative, here it is nominative, parallel to 'dies' as subject
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'temporis' — 'tempus' is nominative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'temporis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'temporis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'temporis' ends in -is

translation: Each one's own day stands fixed; brief and irreparable is time
